It doesn't look bad. The visual style is pretty nice, but a lot of obvious uses of CGI break the illusion. Chalamet seems to lack anything resembling Wilder's manic energy. He seems subdued and laid-back. Another case like Solo and Lone Ranger of showing us how a character "became" who he is by spending the whole movie showing how he used to be completely different in the past? Not a good strategy. The Young Indy scene in Last Crusade showed us how to do this. The young version of the character should have the same personality as the adult version. I'm very happy they kept the Wilder movie's Oompa Loompa design, but making him a mini CGI regular human instead of an actual dwarf is just a much less effective way of doing it. Hollywood taking jobs away from a minority again. This trailer greatly minimizes the female "sidekick" character and doesn't even give her a line. I think she had a much bigger appearance in the first trailer. Another case of hiding the part of the movie that isn't testing well, I assume. I expect the movie will do very well because it's at least "different" from most of the kiddie movies we've been getting lately, and the brand proved extremely durable when the Burton remake was a smash hit. This one is at least making the right choice to tie closer in to the far superior original movie, which I think is the one that has "won the battle" in the pop culture for the definitive version.
